findings.summary: AC4's grep clause ('no event name is hardcoded in loop.py') is enforced by test only against _recorded_terminal; _director_note (loop.py:153) legitimately names 'director_resume' because the note-render is inherently the director channel's own feature (not the shared un-stick rule, which is correctly table-driven). Worth a one-line comment distinguishing 'the rule' (table-driven) from 'the note render' (event-specific) so a future reader doesn't mistake it for the bespoke-if the spec warns against.

test_assessment: AC1-3,5 covered by test_terminals.py + test_loop_director_resume.py (parametrized un-stick, order/one-run, PATH_GUARD_VIOLATION, prefix). AC4 covered by a table-driven test + source-grep on _recorded_terminal. AC6 covered (derive unchanged). AC7 covered by pure _director_note tests plus integration test_note_and_verdict_both_reach_developer_then_self_clear. AC8/AC9 covered by CLI refusal tests (each asserting nothing written) + happy-path stub. AC10 covered (receipt + trace exclusion). AC11 covered by source-inspection guard. AC12 covered (crash-safe). AC13 verified (729 passed, ruff clean, basedpyright 0 errors). Critically, the previously-missing end-to-end coverage now exists: _resume_developer_point has direct unit tests (over 'done'/'cap_reached'/'deadlock', the no-op path, and self-clear) AND full-loop integration tests driving orch.run() for CAP_REACHED, PUSHED, ESCALATED_DEADLOCK, and the flagship MERGE_DECIDED:stop_before_merge (policy-enabled, two real runs) — each asserting exactly one developer round runs, the note is delivered, and the task converges instead of re-terminating. Every new function now has failure-mode coverage.
residual_risks: _resume_developer_point runs on every loop iteration for every task; it is a no-op (returns None) whenever no unconsumed director_resume exists, so normal runs are unaffected, but it is a central hot path (hence risk_level high).

One-event-one-run is preserved by design: the forced developer round sets round=rounds_used+1, so if the resumed run fails to converge it re-caps/re-deadlocks at its next terminal and a second --resume is required — intended and tested, but the Director must understand a single resume buys exactly one run.

Trust surface holds: director_resume/spec_sha are inert in gate_states_from_log and _decide_merge; the resumed round changes code_sha and re-triggers SHA-keyed rw1/rw2/authoritative review, so no stale approval can launder a merge; the resume CLI neither pushes to origin nor merges nor skips a reviewer; a forged resume only buys a compromised VPS more of its own compute.
